header.headline: Ethernet Topology
header.subtitle: Topology maps are architecture diagrams when the system question is really about links, observers, and chokepoints.

hosts.title: Host Nodes
hosts.body: Servers emit requests and consume results across a shared transport fabric.

fabric.title: Fabric Spine
fabric.body: The network core carries traffic and exposes the active bottlenecks.

observers.title: Observers
observers.body: Monitoring points watch latency, drops, and congestion before they become folklore.

actions.title: Control Actions
actions.body: Route updates and queue policy changes act on what the observers find.

outcome.title: Network Posture
outcome.body: The architecture is healthy when links, observers, and control actions line up cleanly.

footer.note: Topology diagrams belong in the training set because they teach structural routing, not just decorative style.
